title: Delete Data Stream
content:
**Reference:** Field | Description ---------------------|------------------------------------------ Name | Unique name identifier. It must contain only lowercase alphanumeric characters. The characters `.`, `_` and `-` are allowed to separate words instead of a space BUT can not be at the beginning or end of the name. Title | The display name to show on the Kelvin UI. It can contain any characters, including spaces. Description | Optional description for the data stream, up to 200 characters. Type | Yes | Determines the type of data, see [overview explanations](../../../overview/concepts/data-stream.md#data-type) for more details. Allowed values for SDK/API: **measurement**, **computed**, **data_quality**, **set_point**. Semantic Type | No | Provides context or deeper meaning behind the data, beyond just its format or structure. Check Kelvin API for the full list of available semantic types. Data Type | Yes | Specifies the kind of data in the stream. Allowed values for SDK/API: **boolean**, **number**, **object**, **string**. Unit | Defines the measurement unit for data. Check Kelvin API for the full list of available units. ## Delete Data Stream Deleting a Data Stream is very simple. The only information you need is the actual `name` of the Data Stream.
=== "Kelvin UI" You can watch this short demo video or read the full step-by step written tutorial below. You can delete Data Streams using the delete button. Simply go to the Data Streams page, select the Data Stream to delete and click the **Delete** button. Then confirm the delete action by typing in **PERMANENTLY DELETE** into the popup confirmation. === "Kelvin API" ```bash title="API cURL Example" linenums="1" curl -X "POST" \ "https:///api/v4/datastreams/doc_demo_data_stream/delete" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer " \ -H "Accept: application/json" \ -d '' ``` === "Kelvin API Client (Python)" ```python title="API Client (Python) Example" linenums="1" from kelvin.api.client import Client # Login client = Client(url="https://", username="") client.login(password="") # Delete Data Stream response = client.datastreams.delete_data_stream(data_stream_name='doc_demo_data_stream') print(response) ```

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api.client import Client

    # Login
    client = Client(url="https://<url.kelvin.ai>", username="<your_username>")
    client.login(password="<your_password>")

    # Delete Data Stream
    response = client.datastreams.delete_data_stream(data_stream_name='doc_demo_data_stream')

    print(response)
code_examples.title: API Client (Python) Example

    print(response)
